text.title: testing-js-ts-frontend Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for testing-js-ts-frontend as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent forgets to import describe/it/expect from 'vitest' when the project is on vitest without globals:true.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leads to silent global pollution or TypeScript errors. Explicitly include the import requirement in every vitest prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agent queries by CSS class (container.querySelector('.btn')) instead of accessible role.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CSS class tests break on style refactors without catching real regressions. Always specify the query priority in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses jest.fn() in a vitest project or vi.fn() in a jest project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The mock API name must match the test runner. Pin the runner name in every prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agent writes snapshot tests for every component as the default strategy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Snapshots are useful for serialisers but brittle for UI components; reviewable diffs only — never approve a 500-line snapshot blindly. Require explicit justification for any snapshot test.
